title: Head of a White Woman Winking by Edward Taylor — Love Poetry Lines
content:
She has one good bumblebee
which she leads about town
on a leash of clover.
It's as big as a Saint Bernard
but also extremely fragile.
People want to pet its long, shaggy coat.
These would be mostly whirling dervishes
out shopping for accessories.
When Lily winks they understand everything,
right down to the particle
of a butterfly's wing lodged
in her last good eye,
meaning: "Head of a White Woman Winking" by Edward Taylor is a love english poem consisting of 19 lines. This English poem by Edward Taylor demonstrates the timeless power of verse to capture complex human emotions. Beginning with "She has one good bumblebee which she leads about town...", this piece explores themes of love through vivid imagery and emotional resonance. The work invites contemplation on the deeper currents of life, love, and the human condition. Edward Taylor's celebrated body of poetry continues to inspire readers across generations and cultures, and this particular work stands as a powerful example of their artistic vision.
